fields.title: Magor Depressive Disorder
fields.short_desc: Write a short note to your future self, reminding them that healing takes time.
fields.detailed_desc:
Write to your future self: Take a note and write a short message to yourself 3 or 6 months from now — remind yourself that healing takes time and small steps count.
Deep breathing: Sit quietly, take 5 slow breaths. Inhale for 3 counts, exhale slowly.

fields.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder
fields.short_desc: Spend two minutes organizing around you; order can make your mind feel safe.
fields.detailed_desc:
Organize a small area: Spend 2 minutes arranging something simple like your desk or a small shelf — structure helps calm sensory overload
Calming sounds: Listen to gentle, slow music or nature sounds while sitting quietly.

fields.title: Loneliness
fields.short_desc: Listen to a song that makes you feel connected or brings good memories.
fields.detailed_desc:
Send a kind message: Text someone you care about — “I thought of you today.” Simple connection helps reduce loneliness.
Journal your feelings: Write “I feel…” and “I wish…” — express your emotions without judging them.

fields.title: Bipolar Disorder
fields.short_desc: Draw or color something that matches how your mood feels right now.
fields.detailed_desc:
Mood tracking: Write “Mood right now:” and “Energy level:” — this helps notice mood patterns.
Short walk or stretching: Move your body gently to release stress and regulate emotions.

fields.title: Anxiety
fields.short_desc: Hold something small in your hand and describe it in detail — focus on its texture, color, and shape.
fields.detailed_desc:
5-4-3-2-1 grounding: Name 5 things you see, 4 you feel, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste — brings you back to the present moment.
Box breathing: Inhale 4 counts, hold 4, exhale 4 — repeat for a minute.

fields.title: PTSD
fields.short_desc: Wrap yourself in a blanket or hug a pillow — remind your body it’s safe now.
fields.detailed_desc:
Name 3 safe things: Look around and identify “something safe I see,” “something safe I can touch,” “something safe I hear.”
Nature sounds: Play calming sounds (waves, rain, forest) for 2–3 minutes — focus only on the sound.

fields.title: Sleeping Disorder
fields.short_desc: Write down three thoughts from your mind before bed to clear your head.
fields.detailed_desc:
No screens before bed: Turn off screens 15 minutes before sleeping and lower the lights.
Bedtime relaxation: Lie down, breathe slowly, and listen to soft sounds or a sleep meditation.

fields.title: Psychotic Depression
fields.short_desc: Say out loud one sentence that keeps you grounded in reality, like “I’m safe in my room right now”.
fields.detailed_desc:
Reality check list: Write two lines — “This is real:” and “This is just a thought:” — helps you separate facts from mental noise.
Ground through touch: Hold a chair or feel the floor — focus on physical sensation.

fields.title: Eating Disorder
fields.short_desc: Before your next meal, take one deep breath and thank your body for supporting you.
fields.detailed_desc:
Mindful gratitude: Before eating, pause and say “I thank my body for working today.
Slow eating: Focus on the taste, smell, and texture of each bite — without judgment.

fields.title: ADHD
fields.short_desc: Pick one simple task and set a fun 5-minute timer — focus until it ends.
fields.detailed_desc:
10-minute timer: Set a timer for 10 minutes and focus on one small task only.
Top 3 goals: Write three things you want to achieve today 
 — keeps your focus structured

fields.title: PDD
fields.short_desc: Stand near a window and notice one thing that looks alive — light, a tree, or movement.
fields.detailed_desc:
Move your body: Stretch or walk for 2 minutes to activate energy.
Set one tiny goal: “Drink an extra glass of water” or “Read one page” — celebrate completing it.

fields.title: OCD
fields.short_desc: When the urge comes, gently tap your fingers five times instead — remind yourself you’re in control.
fields.detailed_desc:
Delay the urge: When you feel a compulsion, breathe slowly for 2 minutes before acting.
 Focus on one sound: Pick a soft noise (fan, clock, or music) and listen to it carefully for a few minutes.
